text.title: Conflict Resolution Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding conflict-resolution. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The Observation field MUST contain only describable facts (dates, quotes, behaviors). Evaluations ('you were rude', 'you don't care') are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Evaluations trigger defense; observations let the listener engage with reality.

rule.statement: The Feeling field MUST use first-person ownership ('I feel X') and avoid pseudo-feelings disguised as accusations ('I feel attacked' = blame masked as feeling).
rule.rationale: Owned feelings invite empathy; pseudo-feelings restart the fight under a fig leaf.

rule.statement: The Need field MUST name a universal need (autonomy, clarity, respect, safety) rather than a specific request masquerading as a need.
rule.rationale: Universal needs are sharable; specific requests-as-needs feel non-negotiable and shut down options.

rule.statement: The Request field MUST be specific, positive, and time-bounded ('Would you confirm by Friday' not 'be more responsive').
rule.rationale: Vague requests cannot be acted on; positive framing avoids 'don't do X' framings which fail to specify what to do instead.

rule.statement: Thomas-Kilmann mode MUST be selected by stakes + relationship importance: high-both → Collaborating; low-low → Avoiding; high-stakes / low-relationship → Competing; low-stakes / high-relationship → Accommodating; equal → Compromising.
rule.rationale: Default-Collaborating wastes effort on small stakes; default-Competing wrecks high-relationship partnerships.
